name: Outback Steakhouse Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients 20 seconds on high and then 20 seconds on low.
Makes 1 quart.

name: Lemon Cucumber Dressing
directions: Blend in blender all ingredients, except sunflower seeds.
While blending, add sunflower seeds.

similar.name: Fat Free Liquid Gold Salad Dressing

name: Magic Mayonnaise
directions: In small mixer bowl, combine all ingredients; beat.
until mixture thickens, about 2 minutes.
Chill to blend flavors.
Store leftovers in a clean jar.

name: Homemade Miracle Whip
directions: In a blender combine vegetable oil, lemon juice, salt, dry or prepared mustard,  and the egg/water mixture.
Blend well.
In a medium sauce pan cook together flour, sugar, water/egg/oil mixture and vinegar until stiff, stirring constantly.
While hot, add to blender.
Pulse a few times and then blend until smooth.
*** Make sure you release the lid a few times during this pulsing to release any built up heat.

name: Low-Cal Salad Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients in blender or food processor on medium speed until fully blended.
Chill thoroughly before serving.

name: Cilantro Dressing
directions: Add the first five ingredients to the blender or food processor and puree until chopped add the yogurt and sour cream and blend until smooth, adding as much salt and pepper as you like.

name: Mom's French Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender or food processor.
Blend till smooth.
Pour into airtight container and refrigerate till ready to use.

name: Low-Fat Creamy Ranch Salad Dressing
directions: In a food processor or blender, process cottage cheese and yogurt until smooth.
Pour into bowl and stir in onions and seasonings.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes so flavors blend.
Store for up to one week.
Stir before serving.

similar.name: Low Fat Honey Dijon Salad Dressing

name: Tofu-Carrot Ginger Dressing with Miso
directions: Place all ingredients in a blender or food processor; process until smooth.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: With an electric mixer beat the eggs till they are light and bouncy.
SLOWLY drizzle the oil and emulsify with the eggs.
SLOWLY.
Add parmessan and rest of the ingredients.
Refridgerate.
NOTE: If using raw eggs use the dressing as soon as possible.

name: Pollis House Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in blender until very smooth.
Store in jar in refrigerator.
Enjoy!

name: Creamy Chili Dressing
directions: Whisk together in a small bowl or combine in a blender the olive oil and sour cream.
Add and whisk together or blend to combine the lime juice, cilantro, chili powder and cumin.
Taste and adjust the seasonings.
Use immediately or cover and refrigerate.

name: Restaurant Style Ranch Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients well and chill for about an hour (I used a food processor to mix).

name: Awesome & Easy Caesar Dressing
directions: Throw all ingredients into your blender and blend for about 30 seconds.
I usually have a taste, on a piece of romaine, and adjust at this point.
(You may like it a bit cheesier or more lemony etc).

name: Aunt Annie's Honey French Dressing
directions: Mix dry ingredients together.
Blend in honey, vinegar, lemon juice, and onion with dry ingredients.
Add oil in steady stream while blending in a blender or with electric beater.

name: Great Salad Dressing
directions: Put all the ingredients in a food processor or blender.
Blend well until combined.
Store in a sealed container in the refrigerator.
Use as needed.

name: Blender Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Add all ingredients to blender jar.
Cover and blend until thick for two minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning.
Refrigerate and use within day or two.

name: Blue Cheese Vinaigrette
directions: Heat 1 T oil in a small, heavy skillet over medium heat; add garlic and saute until golden, about 1 minute.
Transfer garlic mixture to a blender or food processor; add blue cheese, white wine vinegar, water, sugar, hot pepper sauce, salt, pepper and remaining 6 T of olive oil.
Blend well.
Transfer vinaigrette to a bowl; mix in chopped basil.
Can be prepared 2 days ahead, cover and refrigerate.

name: French Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients into blender, cover and process until blended.

name: Cilantro Salad Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in blender/food proccessor and process until smooth.
You could also chop a small amount of cilantro and put it in after your dressing has been blended for"chunks" of cilantro.
Store in the refrigerator.
This is one of those things that gets better as the flavors are allowed to blend, so you probably want to make ahead of time.

name: Japanese Ginger Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in a blender and blend until emulsified.
Stir and shake before each use.

name: Hidden Valley Ranch Buttermilk Dressing Mix
directions: In a blender puree crackers to a fine powder.
Add the parsley, onion and dill.
Pulse to almost dust.
Pour into a bowl and add the remaining ingredients.
Mix well and store for up to 1 year in an airtight container.
to use~ Add 1 and 1/2 heaping T's to 1 cup mayo and 1 cup buttermilk.
Mix well and serve.

name: Fat Free Liquid Gold Salad Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients until smooth.
Store in a jar in the fridge.

name: Sun-Dried Tomato Dressing
directions: Char bell pepper over gas flame or in a broler until blackened all over.
Enclose in a paper bag and let stand 10 minutes.
Peel and seed; coarsely chop.
Combine bell pepper and next 5 ingredients ina food processor.
Blend until almost smooth.
Transfer to a medium bowl.
Mix sun dried tomatoes, 3/4 cup water, cream, vinegar and lemon juice in food processor.
With machine running, add oil; blend until almost smooth.
Whisk sun dried tomato mixture into bell pepper mixture.

similar.name: Omg!! Low Cal Sun-Dried Tomato Dressing

name: Shallot Vinaigrette
directions: Combine ingredients in a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Chill and serve over salad.

name: Sweet and Sour Dressing
directions: Whisk ingredients together or combine in blender, adding oil slowly.
Use on salads as desired.

name: Japanese Ume Dressing
directions: Puree and chill.

name: Japanese Ume Dressing (version 2)
directions: Puree and chill.

name: Dill Salad Dressing
directions: Whiz all ingredients in a blender.
Serve over simple greens only salads.

similar.name: Paula's Pretty in Pink Salad Dressing

name: Tangy Salad dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients except oil in a blender and blend well.
While machine is running pour in oil gradually until combined and dressing is creamy.
Transfer to jar with lid and refrigerate before using.
Shake jar before serving.

name: Tart Olive Oil Mayonnaise
directions: Have ready: An electric beater.
A narrow bowl not much bigger than the diameter of the beaters.
Put the egg yolks into the bowl and beat briefly just to break up.
Add the salt and any optional ingredients.
Add 1 tablespoon of the lemon juice.
Turn on the beater to a medium fast speed.
Dribble the oil so that it is blended into the yolks before adding more.
Continue gradually until all the oil is used.
Gradually add some or all of the remaining lemon juice to taste (I use it all).
HINT: I like to triple the recipe and then store it in a 40 oz.
Jiff Peanut Butter Jar like sold at Sam's Club.

name: Herbed Avocado Dressing
directions: In a blender, mix all ingredients at high speed for 1 minute.
Pour into a container and refrigerate until needed.
Keeps in refrigerator 2 days.

similar.name: Fat Free Liquid Gold Salad Dressing

name: Regas Restaurant Sweet Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: In Hobart mixing bowl, combine all ingredients except canola oil and mix with whip on speed#2 until well mixed.
While continuing to mix, slowly add canola oil until all is incorporated.
Store in a covered cambro, label, date, initial and refrigerate.

name: Roquefort Dressing
directions: The night before you want to make the dressing, line a colander with a wet paper towel, stir the yogurt well, and place in the colander to drain.
Cover with another damp paper towel, and allow to drain overnight.
If you are a wuss, place the colander in a bowl, and let it drain in the fridge; if not, just leave it in the sink overnight.
Yogurt just gets better when in sets at room temperature; its a living organism; it doesnt like the cold any more than you do!
The yogurt should have drained down to about 2 cups, more or less.

If you are a purist, finely chop the scallions, parsley, white onion, and press the garlic; if not, coarsely chop them, throw them in the food processor, pulse until everything is finely chopped, scraping bowl down once, and transfer to a bowl.
At the Magic Lamp, everything but the mayo and seasonings, went through the meat grinder, which still left a little texture.
Combine the yogurt, cream cheese, mayonnaise, and blue cheese in a food processor, or whisk until well combined.
Crumble the Roquefort or additional blue cheese, add to the processor, and pulse a couple more times, or finely crumble, and whisk into the mixture.
Add seasonings, and pulse or whisk until incorporated.
Allow to set for at least 30 minutes to allow flavors to develop, taste, and adjust any or all seasonings to taste.
If possible, refrigerate overnight before using.
Note: If the dressing seems to need something after setting, I often stir in a couple splashes of balsamic vinegar.
This is definitely not part of the original recipe.
Who had ever even heard of balsamic vinegar in the 60s?

name: Roasted Garlic Caesar- Style Dressing
directions: Place the roasted garlic in a small food processor and process until smooth.
Add in all remaining ingredients, and process until well combined.
Adjust ingredients to taste.
Delicious!

name: Cilantro and Green Chile Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients into a blender.
Process until coarsely chopped.
Refrigerate for 1 hour.

name: Restaurant Style Bleu Cheese Dressing
directions: In a small bowl mix together bleu cheese, half & half, garlic powder, salt, and pepper until creamy.
Blend in the sour cream and mayonnaise.
This is best if you allow it to refrigerate for several hours for the flavors to blend before serving.

similar.name: Mom's Top Secret Bleu Cheese Dressing

name: Creamy Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Blend for 1 minute.
Refrigerate for 1 hour before serving.

name: Catalina-Style Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients except oil in a blender and blend together until well combined.  Add oil very slowly while blending.
Blend until dressing is thick and creamy.
Store in covered conatiner in the refrigerator.

name: Blender Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: In blender add egg, cheese, lemon juice, garlic, Worcestershire sauce, mustard, tabasco, salt and pepper.
Blend until combined.
Remove top and with blender on, on lowest speed, gradually add oil in thin stream until thick and creamy.
Pour into jar, cover and refrigerate 1 hours or up to 5 days.  Let stand at room temperature 15 minutes before serving.

name: Sweetened Condensed Milk Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a food processor and combine for a minute or until everything is well mixed.
Pour into bottles.
Makes approx 750ml.

name: Spiced Salad Dressing
directions: Blend in a blender.

name: Thousand Island Dressing (Lower Fat)
directions: In a food processor or blender, process all the ingredients except the salt and pepper and sugar (starting with the listed amounts) process until well combined.
Add in more water to thin if desired and adjust all ingredients to taste.
Season with salt and pepper and sugar if desired.
Transfer to a tightly covered glass jar and store in the refrigerator for 1 week.

name: Avocado Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a food processor and blend until smooth. Chill and serve.

name: Creamy Blue (Bleu) Cheese Dressing
directions: Process all ingredients except blue cheese in food processor until smooth.
Empty ingredients into bowl and mix in blue cheese.  (You can also process the blue cheese in the food processor, but I like my dressing chunky.).
Taste and adjust seasonings.
Cover and refrigerate.

similar.name: Mom's Top Secret Bleu Cheese Dressing

name: Makeover Honey French Dressing
directions: In a blender or food processor, combine the first nine ingredients; cover and process until blended. While processing, add oil in a steady stream. Process until thickened. Store in the refrigerator.

similar.name: Low Fat Honey Dijon Salad Dressing

name: Honey French Dressing
directions: In a blender or food processor, combine the first eight ingredients; cover and process until blended. While processing, add oil in a steady stream. Process until thickened. Store in the refrigerator.

name: Basil Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: Add everything except olive oil to a blender and blend while streaming in olive oil.

name: Easy Blender Mayonnaise
directions: crack egg (discard shell), mustard powder, salt, sugar, cayenne pepper, and olive oil (or other oil) in a blender and blend on high until well combined.
remove cover or through the additions opening slowly add 1/2 cup of vegetable oil.
then add the juice of one lemon (or the vinegar).
once the mixture is well combined, slowly add the remaining 1/2 cup vegetable oil.
the mixture should be well mixed and thick. It will be thinner then you find it at the store but it will thicken further when it is cooled in the fridge.
Store in the refrigerator in a clean jar.
Keeps for about one week.

name: Dilled Sour Cream Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients into blender container.
Cover and process High speed until onion is finely grated.

name: Cilantro and Jalapeno Ranch Dressing
directions: Mix the dressing mix, milk (buttermilk if using) and mayonnaise until well blended.
Let sit until thickened in fridge, about 30 minutes.
Put dressing in a blender, and add jalapenos and cilantro and puree all together.

name: Low Fat Honey Dijon Salad Dressing
directions: Mix tofu, mustard, and honey in a food processor.
Slow drizzle in soy milk til dressing reaches desired consistency.
Refrigerate immediately to blend flavors.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: This dressing is easily made in a blender. Blend together the honey, mustard, and vinegar.
Start with ¼ cup of vinegar. Some brands have a harsher taste than others.
With the blender running on the 2nd or 3rd setting, slowly add the oil until it is completely absorbed and emulsified. A stick blender works well, too.
Add more vinegar, according to your taste. Adjust seasoning to taste.
Blend the dressing one last time before pouring into a container.
Store leftovers in the refrigerator. Shake well before using.

name: The Best French Salad Dressing
directions: In a large food processor blend all ingredients together until smooth (starting with 1/2 teaspoon garlic salt and 1/2 cup sugar and add in more to taste after mixing.
Do not attempt to make this in a small 4-cup blender or you will have the dressing all over your counter!
Mix in the mayonnaise (if using) for a creamy-style French dressing.
Place in the fridge for a couple of hours before serving.

name: Kalyn's Chipotle Ranch Dressing
directions: Put salsa in food processor and puree about one minute with steel blade. (Could use a blender or immersion blender.).
Add ranch dressing and ground chipotle and process 30 seconds more, or until well combined.

name: Garlic and Olive Salad Dressing (Tofu)
directions: Put all ingredients but tofu and water into food processor.  Process until all are mixed and smooth.
Add water and tofu and blend some more.
Put into container and refrigerate for up to 10 days.

name: Dairy-Free Ranch Dressing (Tofu)
directions: Put all ingredients into the blender except for the 2-3 tablespoons water.
Blend on a slow speed and add the additional water a tablespoon at a time until using a rubber spatula you can get all to blend well until creamy and smooth.
Put in a covered container or jar and refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
Store refrigerated for up to 10 days.

name: Mom's Top Secret Bleu Cheese Dressing
directions: Cook sugar and water until bubbles are the size of a dime.
Remove from heat and cool.
Pour into blender.
Add remaining ingredients and blend thoroughly.
Add bleu cheese and garlic, leave a couple of chunks of the cheese as crumbles to add after blending.

name: Avocado Dressing for Taco Salad
directions: Start with garlc clove, onion powder, red pepper sauce and lemon or lime juice, blend until it's a paste;  I use either a stick blender or magic bullet.
Add  2 avocados, blend until smooth.
Add salad oil, blend.
Chop 3rd avocado (if you want dressing to be chunky).
Add 3rd avocado and sour cream, mix by hand.

name: Simple Gorgonzola Cheese Salad Dressing
directions: Crunble gorgonzola cheese into a food processor and add vinegar; run the machine until mixture is smooth.
With the machine running,(DO NOT ADD OIL ALL AT ONCE)Add oil in a thin stream very slowly to emulsify.
Taste, if needed add some salt.
Tranfer oil to small jar; Serve over mixed greens & fruit.

name: Best Oriental Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender and blend at medium speed 3-4 seconds until celery is finely grated.
This can be served immediately, but the flavors will blend better if refrigerated about an hour before serving.

The fewer substitutions you have to make, the better, of course, but I've used each of these at one time or other, and still been happy with the results.  Send me any other substitutions you've tried!

name: Jer's Vinaigrette
directions: combine all ingredients except oil in cuisinart or blender.
pulse until everything is pureed evenly.
with processor running drizzle oil in a steady thin stream until all is added.  If oil begins to pool, slow down stream.

name: Green Apple Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a blender except for the olive oil, and apples. Blend until smooth.
Slowly add the olive oil and blend well.
Remove vinaigrette from blender and add the apples, mix and serve on salad greens.

similar.name: Caramelized Apple Salad With Spicy Orange Vinaigrette
Fat Free Liquid Gold Salad Dressing

name: Caramelized Apple Salad With Spicy Orange Vinaigrette
directions: For the Vinaigrette:
Place orange juice in a small nonreactive pan over high heat and cook until reduced to 1/4 cup.
Place reduce orange juice, vinegar, onion, jalapeno and ancho chili powdeer in a blender and blend until smooth.  With the motor running, slowly add the olive oil until emulsified.
Add honey and the salt and pepper to taste.
For the Apple Salad:

Heat 3/4 cup of the spicy Orange vinaigrette in a medium saute pan, over medium high heat.  Add the apples, cut-side down and saute until golden brown.  Turn over and saute until just cooked trough, 1 to 2  minutes.
Place the water cress and fresee in a medium bowl and toss with a few tablespoons of the remaining Spicy Orange Vinagirettte, season with salt and pepper and divide among 4 plates.
Arrange 2 apple quarters on each plate, garanish with walnuts and feta cheese and drizzle with some of the remaining vinaigrette.

similar.name: Regas Restaurant Sweet Vinaigrette Salad Dressing

name: $10 Dressing
directions: Combine ingredients in blender. Enjoy!

name: Omg!! Low Cal Sun-Dried Tomato Dressing
directions: In food processor mince garlic.
Add tomatoes, basil leaves, and chipotle; mince.
Add balsamic.
Add mayo, pepper and  water to desired consistency.

name: Kittencal's Oil-Based Caesar Dressing (With Egg)
directions: In a food processor process the garlic with anchovies until minced (if you have a small 4-cup processor then use it for this).
Add in all remaining ingredients (except the Parmesan cheese and black pepper) and process until smooth and blended (about 10 seconds).
Add in parmesan cheese; pulse a few times.
Season with fresh ground pepper to taste.
Cover and refrigerate until ready to use.
Mix well before using.

name: Low-Fat Celery Seed Dressing
directions: To make the vegetable broth oil substitute:
Bring a cup of water to boil, add the broth powder and mix well.
Reserve 2 tablespoons of the broth and set aside.
Off heat, add 1 tablespoon of cornstarch to the veg broth, stirring quickly so the cornstarch dissolves.
Take 2 teaspoons of cornstarch and add to the reserved veg broth, stirring quickly.
Back on heat, stir in the new cornstarch broth mixture to make a gel.
Let cool in fridge. (This makes it into a sort of paste).
To make the chicken broth oil substitute, repeat steps 3-7. The chicken broth mixture isn't as thick and pasty.

Once the broth is cool, add all the ingredients and blend in a blender. The consistency isn't as thick as the original, but it's not as bad as just using water instead of oil. The consistency without the water is much better, but the taste wasn't quite on.

I know it seems like a lot steps - if you want to choose just the chicken broth mixture instead of both the chicken and veg broth or vice versa, I'm sure it would be fine, I was just trying to find the best taste! Like I said -- still working on it!

name: Amanda's Ginger Orange Dressing
directions: Place all the ingredients in a blender jar or mason jar that screws onto your blender. Process until the garlic and ginger are totally blended up.
Keeps in the fridge for months and months and months.

name: Creamy Feta Dressing
directions: Combine yogurt, cheese and oil.  Mix well.
Stir in basil, vinegar, salt and pepper.
Chill.
Alternatively-Put every thing in the blender and let 'er rip.

name: Autumn Apple Cider Vinaigrette
directions: With blender, mix cider vinegar, apple juice, brown sugar, black pepper, cayenne, cinnamon and salt.  Blend until all ingredients are incorporated. Continue mixing on low speed and add oil slowly until emulsification starts.  Change speed to medium and add remaining oil. Adjust seasoning.

similar.name: Caramelized Apple Salad With Spicy Orange Vinaigrette

name: Easy Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients into a blender or use a hand blender and blend until emusified.  Refrigerate for several hours before use.  Cook time is chill time.

name: Leah's Pesto Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in food processor, making sure to at least peel the paper off of the garlic cloves.
Pulse to desired consistency, tasting as you go, adding ingredients to taste.
All amounts except basil leaves are guesstimates, as I make this almost every morning to go on my lunchtime salad.

I have used peanuts, pecans, hickory nuts, and the classic pine nuts.  It's all good.  Whatever you have on hand will work just fine.  I like it to have a distinguishable zing, those with less tolerable palates may want to just use one garlic clove.

name: Peanut-Lime Vinaigrette
directions: Place all ingredients but the oil and salt in a food processor. Pulse until everything is combined.
Drizzle in the oil with the processor on low.
Scrape the sides of the bowl and chill for half hour before serving.
Goes great on noodles or a light salad.

name: Low Fat Ranch Dressing
directions: Mix cottage cheese and dressing packet in your blender.
Add water, blending until desired consistency.
**UPDATE:  I now use 16 ounces of the cottage cheese and use less water.

similar.name: Hidden Valley Ranch Buttermilk Dressing Mix
Low Fat Honey Dijon Salad Dressing

name: Healthy Cilantro Lime Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Place all EXCEPT cilantro in a blender and blend until smooth.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and serve over a fresh salad.

name: Green Goddess Dressing
directions: Put everything in food processor and process until smooth.
Transer to a covered jar and serve in teh fridge for up to 5 months.

name: Slaw Dressing (Without Oil)
directions: Combine the first 3 ingredients in a micro safe bowl.
Microwave until boiling.
Allow to cool, then add the remaining ingredients,
Mix well.
Pour over shredded cabbage/slaw mix.

name: Cilantro-Lime Salad Dressing
directions: Mix altogether in a blender.

name: Raspberry Vinaigrette
directions: In a blender combine all ingredients.
Puree until smooth.

name: Cranberry Vinaigrette
directions: Put cranberries into cup with 1/4 cup of cranberry juice and allow them to plump, about 20 minutes.
Place 1 1/4 cups of cranberry juice in a sauce pan and bring to a boil.
Continue to boil over high heat until liquid is reduced to a half cup, about 10 minutes.
Drain cranberries, and throw away the juice.
Whisk together the cranberries, reduction and remaining ingredients until smooth.  It will be a bit clumpy but add texture to salad.

name: Green Goddess Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and blend until smooth.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing (No Egg)
directions: In a blender, combine all the ingredients EXCEPT the olive oil.
Next, while the blender is running, and slowly add olive oil.
Chill 20-60 minutes.

name: Paula's Pretty in Pink Salad Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients into a tall container and blend with an immersion blender until emulsified.  Refrigerate until ready to serve.

Notes: I used a KitchenAid immersion blender on setting 1.  Also, you don't have to use a stick blender.  If you don't care about the dressing emulsifying, you can shake everything together in a tightly sealed jar or mix in a regular blender.  In the original recipe, Paula used vegetable oil and just called for regular black pepper.

name: Basil Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all of the ingredients, except oil, in a food processor and blend.  Slowly add olive oil in a slow, thin, stream, until creamy.
Season to taste and serve, or refrigerate up to 3 days.

name: Honey Lime Vinegarette
directions: Place all ingredients except oil in a blender and blend until smooth.
Slowly add the oil until it's well incorporated.
Chill or server immediately over mixed greens or salad of your choice.

name: Summer Vinaigrette
directions: In blender or food processor combine basil, garlic, lemon juice,salt, pepper and balsamic vinegar.
Process to until well blended.
While motor slowly add oil.
Taste to see if you need to add salt or pepper.
Enjoy the taste of summer.

name: Dried Cranberry Vinaigrette
directions: Combine the cranberry juice and dried cranberries in microwave-safe bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and microwave on high power until hot, about 1 minute.
Process hot cranberry mixture, vinegar, shallot, garlic, thyme, mustard, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper in blender until shallot and garlic are finely chopped, about 15 seconds.
With blender running, add oil and continue to process until smooth and emulsified, about 15 seconds.
Makes about 1 1/4 cups.

name: Creamy Chipotle-Lime Dressing
directions: Process lime juice, shallot, garlic, chipotle, sugar, cumin, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper in blender until shallot and garlic are finely chopped, about 15 seconds.
Add mayonnaise, sour cream, and cilantro; continue to process until smooth, about 15 seconds.
Makes about 1 1/2 cups.
Store in the refrigerator.

name: Delicious Vinaigrette
directions: Blend all ingredients except the oil and onions in Blender/Food Processor.
Next slowly add the oil, blending it all together thoroughly.
Then add half the onions and again blend.
Finally the last of the onions are chopped finely and whisked into the mixture.
Let stand 2-3 hours - Do Not Refrigerate.

name: Apple Cider Vinegar Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in blender container and process until well combined.  Use immediately or store covered in refrigerator.

name: 55 Salad Dressing
directions: In a blender, mix garlic, mustard, vinegar and water until smooth. .
Add remaining ingredients except oil.
Process on low speed. Slowly add oil. Blend 10 minutes.
